title: wallet/src/db.rs: get_session_by_address()
description:
What: Add get_session_by_address(address: &str) -> Result<Option<PaymentSession>, WalletError> to WalletStore in wallet/src/db.rs.

Algorithm:
1. Execute SELECT * FROM payment_sessions WHERE address = ?1 AND state = 'Watching' LIMIT 1 via self.conn.query_row().
2. Map the rusqlite Row to PaymentSession using the same column mapping as existing session queries.
3. Return Ok(Some(session)) on match, Ok(None) on no rows (map QueryReturnedNoRows -> Ok(None)), Err(WalletError::Db(e)) on other rusqlite errors.

Defensive programming:
- address is untrusted input; pass as query parameter only, never interpolate into SQL.
- LIMIT 1 prevents ambiguity if multiple rows somehow match.
- State filter 'Watching' prevents returning completed/expired sessions.
- Return typed WalletError::Db, never unwrap().

Tests (in wallet/src/db.rs #[cfg(test)]):
- test_get_session_by_address_hit: insert a Watching session with a known address, call get_session_by_address, assert session.id matches.
- test_get_session_by_address_miss: call with address not in DB, assert Ok(None).
- test_get_session_by_address_wrong_state: insert a Complete session, assert Ok(None) (state filter must exclude it).

Acceptance: cargo test -p nie-wallet passes, cargo clippy -p nie-wallet -- -D warnings clean.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-wallet passes; get_session_by_address returns Ok(None) for unknown address and Ok(Some(session)) for Watching sessions; clippy clean
close_reason: get_session_by_address() + sessions_to_watch() added to WalletStore; 7 tests pass; clippy clean
